Class CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext
Enclosing class:
CombinationGeneratorTask
static class CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext extends Object
A container for all thread-local resources used by a generator.

This class is a key part of the resource management strategy. It consolidates all expensive, thread-specific objects into a single container. An instance of this context is stored in a field, ensuring that each thread in the ForkJoinPool has its own set of resource pools and a dedicated WorkBatch.

Optimization Strategy

By fetching this context object only once per task via context.get(), we avoid the performance penalty of multiple ThreadLocal lookups in the hot path. The context is then passed as a parameter to downstream methods, providing fast, contention-free access to:

  • An prefixArrayPool for recycling short[] arrays.
  • A taskPool for recycling CombinationGeneratorTask objects.
  • The currentBatch being filled by the thread.
This pattern is crucial for achieving near-zero allocation during combination generation.

Since:
2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) access time after initial allocation.
Threading Model:
This class is NOT thread-safe, but is intended to be used in a thread-local manner.
Memory Management:
Minimal memory footprint of two fixed-capacity pools and a batch reference.

  • Field Details

    • threadName

      private final String threadName
      The name of the thread owning this context, for logging purposes.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Final Flush Refactor
      See Also:
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.allContexts
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.flushAllPendingBatches()
      • Thread.getName()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of ~20 bytes as a String.

    • prefixArrayPool

      final ArrayPool prefixArrayPool
      A thread-local pool for recycling short[] arrays used for prefixes.
      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      See Also:
      • ArrayPool
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized access time for ArrayPool.get() and ArrayPool.put(short[]).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe, should be used in a thread-local manner.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed footprint of ~4 bytes as a reference.

    • taskPool

      final TaskPool taskPool
      A thread-local pool for recycling CombinationGeneratorTask instances.
      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized access time for TaskPool.get() and TaskPool.put(CombinationGeneratorTask).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe, should be used in a thread-local manner.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed footprint of ~4 bytes as a reference.

    • currentBatch

      WorkBatch currentBatch
      The WorkBatch currently being filled by this thread.

      Each generator thread works on its own batch, which is preserved across multiple tasks executed by that thread. When the batch is full, it is flushed to a queue, and a new, clean batch is obtained from a central pool. Storing the batch here, within the thread-local context, eliminates contention and simplifies batch management.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch.BATCH_SIZE
      • getOrCreateBatch()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access and update time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. References to this batch should not be kept after flushing.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed footprint of ~4 bytes as a reference.

  • Constructor Details

    • GeneratorContext

      GeneratorContext()
      Initializes a new CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext and registers it in the global context list.

      This constructor is meant to be called only by the CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorWorkerThread's initializer, and adds the context to the global list for the final flush.

      Since:
      2025.10 - Final Flush Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized insertion time into the global context list.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe by nature of construction and use of a thread-safe queue.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate, apart from the instance itself.

  • Method Details

    • getThreadName

      public String getThreadName()
      Gets the name of the thread owning this context. Used for logging purposes.
      Returns:
      The name of the this context's thread.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Final Flush Refactor
      See Also:
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.flushAllPendingBatches()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, as it returns an immutable field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns a reference to an existing String.

    • getOrCreateBatch

      WorkBatch getOrCreateBatch()
      Returns the current WorkBatch for this thread, obtaining a new one from the central pool if one doesn't exist.

      This method ensures a generator task always has a valid batch to write to. It performs a fast null check and, if needed, calls the blocking getNewBatchBlocking() method to retrieve a recycled batch instance.

      Returns:
      The current WorkBatch, or null if interrupted while obtaining a batch.
      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) null check and amortized O(1) for batch retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Each thread should manage its own context instance.

    • getNewBatchBlocking

      private WorkBatch getNewBatchBlocking()
      Retrieves a new WorkBatch from the global pool, blocking until one is available.

      When a monkey finishes with a WorkBatch, it returns it to a central pool for recycling. This method retrieves a batch from that pool. It uses a lightweight loop with a relaxedPoll() to ensure the generator thread remains responsive to cancellation signals from the ForkJoinPool, which a standard Thread.onSpinWait() call would prevent.

      The loop now properly checks for thread interruption, allowing the ForkJoinPool to shutdown generator threads when a solution is found. If the thread is interrupted during the spin loop, the method returns null to signal shutdown. The thread also parks briefly to reduce CPU usage during the wait.

      Returns:
      A clean, recycled WorkBatch, or null if the thread was interrupted.
      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      See Also:
      • CombinationQueueArray.getWorkBatchPool()
      • TestClickCombination.triggerGeneratorShutdown()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized access time, spinning if necessary.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe interactions with the global pool, but the method itself is not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses existing batches in the pool.

    • resetBatch

      WorkBatch resetBatch()
      Replaces the currentBatch with a new one obtained from the central pool.

      This is called after the current batch is successfully flushed. It discards the reference to the old batch and retrieves a new, clean one, ensuring the generator thread can immediately start filling it.

      Returns:
      The new WorkBatch or null if interrupted.
      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      See Also:
      • getNewBatchBlocking()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized access time, spinning if necessary.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Each thread should manage its own context instance.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses existing batches in the pool.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
